results.evidence: Multi-bill flow in AC v2 uses a 'Parent-Child' order structure, matching user intent of 'Pay All'.
Terminology uses 'Thanh toán đa hóa đơn' in backend event codes.
UI-specific.
Logic separates the 'Aggregation' phase from the 'Execution' phase in AC.
PE ensures idempotency for the entire batch via a unique batch_request_id.
Transaction history shows the consolidated payment with drill-down to individual bills.
UI-specific.
Timeouts for biller callbacks are handled per bill, allowing partial success/fail states if configured.
System supports 'Select All' defaults for saved bills via Bill Service.
Flow context is preserved during the aggregation of multiple bill codes.
AC session maintains the 'cart' of bills during backgrounding/network loss.
Cancel/Back triggers cleanup of the temporary 'Parent Order' in AC.
UI-specific.
UI-specific.
Aggregation logic is prioritised for high-frequency categories (Electricity, Water).
UI-specific.
Pending bills from different billers are surfaced in a single 'Pending Payments' view.
New users are guided towards single bill payment first to establish trust.
Activation funnel for multi-bill includes biller verification steps.
KYC/Limit checks are performed against the *total* amount of all bills.
Notifications include a summary of the number of bills paid + total amount.
Each biller in the batch is verified against the Merchant Registry.
High-risk mitigation includes RBA triggers for large total batch amounts.
Unusual batch composition (e.g., bills from different provinces) flags risk.
Self-service unblocking for multi-bill failures (due to risk) needs clearer entry points from the batch result page.
AC v2 uses parallel processing for biller callbacks to minimize latency.
Centralised business logic ensures multi-bill works identically on iOS/Android.
Consent for multi-payment and data sharing with multiple billers is captured.
Final confirmation shows the itemised list of bills and the total total.
Available vs. Hold states are maintained per individual bill order in ZAS.
Fees are aggregated but shown itemised if different billers have different fee structures.
Promotions (e.g., 'Pay 3, get discount') are evaluated at the batch level.
Pending biller confirmations are tracked via PENDING_PARTNER state in AC.
Reconciliation for multi-bill is complex; one ZLP txn maps to N biller txns. Requires robust mapping tables.
Limits are checked against the aggregated amount of the whole cart.
SBV disclosures for aggregated payments are provided.
Disputes can be initiated per individual bill in the batch history.
Default SoF (Wallet) is used for the total amount.
User can change SoF if the total amount exceeds Wallet balance.
Unlinking a bank doesn't affect the multi-bill 'cart' if Wallet is used.
Fallback SoF offered if the total batch amount fails on primary source.
Clarity provided on which bills are eligible for multi-payment.
Processing state shows progress of individual bill confirmations.
Result screen clearly shows which bills succeeded and which failed (Partial Success support).
IDs include BatchID + individual OrderIDs for each bill.
If a specific biller is in maintenance, that bill is flagged/removed from the batch early.
Fee/Total confirmation timing is correct (before PIN entry).
Stale bill amounts are refreshed via Biller API before confirmation.
History syncs all bill statuses within seconds of batch completion.
Errors specify which bill failed (e.g., 'Electricity bill code invalid').
If partial success, user is prompted to retry only the failed bills.
Input data for all bills is retained on failure for easy correction.
Idempotency prevents duplicate payment of the same batch or individual bills.
UI-specific.
Degraded mode (e.g., peak load) can limit the maximum number of bills per batch.
OTP for large batches is monitored for delivery success.
Batch context (all OrderIDs) is available to CS agents.
Issues can be routed to specific biller-integration teams if only one bill fails.
Next actions for partial success are clear for CS agents.
Status of each bill in the batch is traceable throughout its lifecycle.
CS contact pre-populates the BatchID for context.
Reconciliation automation is in progress to handle complex N:1 mappings.
Parallel API calls to billers ensure sub-2s total processing time.
All surfaces reflect the updated status of all paid bills.
Callback from biller returns to the Batch Result page.
Key steps (Aggregate, Validate, Reserve, Execute) are logged per bill.
One biller's outage doesn't block the entire batch (soft-fail support).
Rate limits apply to the batch submission API.
Unique BatchID prevents duplicate submission of the same cart.
User is informed if a biller's fee changed since they last paid.
System remembers the 'last paid bills' to suggest a multi-bill batch next month.
Confidence recovery for partial success is handled via 'Retry Failed Only' flow.
UI-specific.
Tone is consistent across itemised result details.
Processing time promises for multi-bill are met through parallel execution.
